cases.expected: Teacher can authenticate and load enterprise coursework overview.
cases.actual: Teacher login succeeded and coursework overview rendered.

steps: Create an enterprise coursework template through the authenticated teacher API
cases.expected: Template and rubric are created for the teacher scope.
cases.actual: Template cmrj9u8d30002z8uw1o5pqjef created with 2 rubric criteria.

steps: Create a draft publication targeted to the eligible student
cases.expected: Publication lifecycle supports valid DRAFT -> SCHEDULED -> PUBLISHED transitions.
cases.actual: Publication cmrj9ud5n000bz8uw7buefjks moved from DRAFT to SCHEDULED to PUBLISHED.

cases.expected: Invalid lifecycle transitions are rejected by the server.
cases.actual: Archived publication rejected invalid reverse transition as expected.

cases.expected: Eligible student can list and open the published targeted coursework.
cases.actual: Eligible student listed publication cmrj9ud5n000bz8uw7buefjks and loaded the submission route.

steps: Attempt to open the targeted coursework detail route
cases.expected: Non-target student is denied access to the targeted coursework.
cases.actual: Foreign student was denied publication visibility and attempts API access.

steps: Submit text, link, repository URL, and attachment through the UI
cases.expected: Student can submit an attempt and the attempt is persisted with an attachment.
cases.actual: Attempt cmrj9v2zz000pz8uweq3s8qg7 submitted with 1 attachment(s).

steps: Repeat the same submission with the same idempotency key
Verify no duplicate attempt or attachment is created
cases.expected: Duplicate submission returns the same submitted attempt without duplicating side effects.
cases.actual: Duplicate submit returned attempt cmrj9v2zz000pz8uweq3s8qg7 idempotently and kept attempt count at 1.

steps: Return the first attempt for revision
Verify history shows both attempts and the limit blocks a third
cases.expected: Returned resubmission creates a second immutable attempt and the attempt limit is enforced.
cases.actual: Returned attempt created a separate second attempt (cmrj9vck80014z8uwo1ikrph9) and a third attempt was rejected at the limit.

steps: Open the protected download route for your own attachment
cases.expected: Only the owning student and authorized teachers can fetch protected coursework attachments.
cases.actual: Protected attachment delivery allowed the owner, denied a foreign student, and rejected anonymous access for attachment cmrj9vcqn0016z8uwir1ex3bm.

steps: Request an extension for the published coursework
cases.expected: Student can create an extension request for eligible coursework.
cases.actual: Extension request cmrj9vmst001cz8uwt3y0lciz created in REQUESTED state.

steps: Approve the latest extension request through the teacher API
cases.expected: Teacher can approve an eligible extension request with a new deadline.
cases.actual: Extension request cmrj9vmst001cz8uwt3y0lciz approved with a new deadline.

steps: Create a publication that is already past due
Approve an extension for the same student
Verify the same student can submit within the extension window
cases.expected: Server-side late-policy enforcement blocks after the original deadline and only the approved extension reopens the deadline for that student.
cases.actual: Past-due submission was blocked before approval, then attempt cmrj9vzyk001tz8uwdxpxiwm2 succeeded only after the approved extension.

steps: Lead teacher saves a draft grade
cases.expected: The moderation chain enforces role separation, keeps draft grades hidden from students, audits each transition, and only exposes the grade after publication.
cases.actual: Moderation chain completed for attempt cmrj9vck80014z8uwo1ikrph9, self-approval was denied, and all six transition audits were recorded.

steps: Open the teacher grading studio after moderation and publication
cases.expected: Teacher can see the published grade state after the moderation workflow completes.
cases.actual: Teacher grading studio displayed the published grade cmrj9w8fe0024z8uwxu5edibm after moderation completed.

steps: Verify workflow notifications created by publish, resubmission, extension, grade publication, submission receipt, and review request paths
cases.expected: Expected coursework notifications exist once for the correct users, with authorized links and no duplicate leakage.
cases.actual: Student notifications (5) and teacher notifications (3) were recorded without duplicate grade-publication side effects.

steps: Open coursework history after grade publication
cases.expected: Student can see published feedback without private teacher notes.
cases.actual: Published feedback was visible and private teacher notes were not exposed to the student.

cases.expected: Teacher reports and CSV export are available for the publication scope.
cases.actual: Teacher report JSON and CSV export both succeeded for the publication scope.

steps: Run critical teacher and student coursework pages in light/dark mode across desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports
Verify no horizontal overflow, no critical console errors, and no failed network requests
cases.expected: Critical coursework workflows remain usable across the required viewport and theme matrix.
cases.actual: Viewport/theme matrix completed across 12 role+viewport combinations with no overflow, console, or network failures.

cases.expected: Closed coursework rejects new submissions.
cases.actual: Closed publication rejected a new submission attempt.

notes: Console error count observed during run: 9
